message:
refactor(install): use if statement instead of || true for cleaner error handling

The previous fix used `|| true` which masked potential rm errors.
Using an explicit if statement is cleaner, doesn't hide errors,
and achieves the same goal of preventing set -e from triggering
on the empty dir check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -29,9 +29,10 @@ TMP_DIRS=()
 cleanup_tmp_dirs() {
     local dir
     for dir in "${TMP_DIRS[@]:-}"; do
-        # Use || true to prevent exit code 1 when TMP_DIRS is empty
-        # (empty string causes [ -n "" ] to fail, which propagates due to set -e)
-        [ -n "$dir" ] && rm -rf "$dir" || true
+        # Use if statement to avoid set -e triggering on empty dir check
+        if [ -n "$dir" ]; then
+            rm -rf "$dir"
+        fi
     done
 }
